question_text: A student lifts a 2 kg book from the ground to a shelf 1.5 m high, then pushes it along the shelf (which is frictionless) for 3 m. Which statement best explains the energy changes that occur?

options.label: A
options.text: Gravitational potential energy increases during lifting; kinetic energy increases during pushing along the shelf

options.label: B
options.text: Gravitational potential energy increases during lifting; no change in gravitational potential energy during pushing as the height stays the same

options.label: C
options.text: Kinetic energy increases during lifting; gravitational potential energy increases during pushing

options.label: D
options.text: Both gravitational potential and kinetic energy increase equally during both stages

explanation: When lifting the book, gravitational potential energy increases because height increases. When pushing along a frictionless horizontal shelf, the height doesn't change, so gravitational potential energy remains constant—only kinetic energy would change if the shelf is frictionless.
